compiled_state.title: As a skill author, I have example evals for representative skills and a smoke test
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Create evals for 2 more representative skills (total 3 with the one from S1: one simple, one complex, one with trigger routing tests). Document a smoke test procedure that demonstrates the framework detecting an intentionally-introduced regression.

**Why**: Example evals serve as templates for skill authors. The smoke test proves the framework works end-to-end and gives new authors a procedure to follow when validating their own evals.

**Scope**:
- IN: 2 additional skill evals (complex skill, trigger routing skill), documented smoke test procedure, regression detection demonstration
- OUT: Evals for all 32 skills (future work), automated smoke test in CI

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, at least 3 representative skills have evals (one simple, one complex, one with trigger routing tests) and a documented smoke test demonstrates the framework detecting an intentionally-introduced regression
  ← Satisfies: "At least 3 representative skills ship with evals and a smoke test demonstrates detecting an intentionally-introduced regression"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Smoke test must use cheapest viable model (Haiku) to minimize API cost
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: All 3 tasks complete — brainstorm eval, fix-bug eval, smoke test docs
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
